mnemonics.title: Incremental Model Phases
mnemonics.content: R-D-D-M: Requirements → Design & Develop → Deploy → Maintain. Remember as 'Rising Dragons Dance Majestically' to visualize the flow.

mnemonics.title: Advantages of Incremental Model
mnemonics.content: FEAR: Flexibility, Early delivery, Adaptive to changes, Risk reduction. Each letter represents a key benefit.

mnemonics.title: When to Use Incremental Model
mnemonics.content: REACT: Requirements may evolve, Early delivery needed, Available skilled resources, Complex project, Time-bound delivery. Remember as 'REACT to changing needs'.

acronyms.usage: An incremental model emphasizing short development cycles and reusable components for fast delivery.
The smallest version of a product that can be released to validate ideas and gather user feedback.
Testing performed when individual increments are combined into the complete system.
Person responsible for planning, executing, and closing the incremental development project.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a staircase where each step represents an increment - each step adds height (new features) while building on previous steps (core functionality).
Think of Iterative as refining a sculpture (same clay, better shape) and Incremental as building with blocks (adding new blocks each time).
Picture concentric circles - each outer circle represents an increment containing everything in inner circles plus new features.

analogies.analogy: Like building a house: first build the foundation and basic structure (first increment), then add rooms, then decorate - each step creates a livable space.
Like video game levels - each level (increment) is complete and playable, but the game gets more features as you progress.
Like a restaurant that starts with a limited menu, gets customer feedback, then adds new dishes based on what customers enjoyed.
